sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, has a non-healing foot ulcer on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, which has been present for approximately two months.
The ulcer has recently become red and is draining.
The patient reports that the ulcer sometimes throbs, but does not have specific pain.
The patient is concerned about the ulcer's healing.
The patient has a history of walking barefoot on the beach, specifically at Dewey Beach, where he thinks he may have gotten the ulcer.
The patient has not picked up his prescribed antibiotics from his primary care physician (PCP) yet.
The patient's diabetes is not well-controlled, with an A1c level of around 10.
The patient takes his diabetes medication but does not regularly check his blood sugar levels.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes.
Review of systems: The patient denies fever.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal.
Physical exam: The patient has a one-by-two inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth metatarsophalangeal joint.
The wound has redness, drainage, and edema, but no necrosis, odor, or bony exposure.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right foot showed no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with a possible infection, likely due to poorly controlled diabetes.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; suspected infection; poorly controlled diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed clindamycin 400mg twice a day for seven days to treat the infection.
I will perform a debridement to remove dead tissue.
I will order an ABI (ankle-brachial index) to assess blood supply to the foot.
I will also obtain a surgical shoe for the patient to wear to reduce pressure on the foot and promote healing.
The patient will follow up in two weeks for another debridement and to reassess the wound.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in two weeks.
